TextENGNew: As previously described, the council of the Three Families has become a hollow formality in the present day -- in practice, it is a dictatorship under the Sonozaki family.
Both the Kimiyoshi and Furude families are shadows of their former glory, and it is difficult to believe they have upheld the ancient traditions of old.
Among them, only the Sonozaki family has preserved its authority from antiquity, inheriting a rich tapestry of traditions dating back to the era when the village was known as Onigafuchi.

Even the most recent confirmed instance of what appears to be a genuine Watanagashi -- an incident at the end of the Meiji era -- is believed to have been orchestrated by the Sonozaki family.@k(Refer to the Three Families genealogical chart from the late Meiji period.)
changeReason: New translation preserves the uncertainty expressed by だと思われる ('what appears to be a genuine Watanagashi'), while the old version treated it as a confirmed 'verifiable example'.

TextENGNew: Since the Meiji era, the Sonozaki family has exercised strong leadership in order to guide the development of Hinamizawa Village.

During the dam conflict several years ago, the Kimiyoshi family held the chairmanship of the opposition alliance, but this was purely nominal -- in truth, the Sonozaki family reigned as the power behind the scenes.
It is whispered that the Kimiyoshi family led the public acts of resistance, while the Sonozaki family carried out those that could not be made public.

Even within Hinamizawa itself, people whisper that the string of disturbing incidents reported during the dam dispute -- including the infamous kidnapping of the Construction Minister's son -- were the work of the Sonozaki family.
Furthermore, it is said that the Sonozaki family's covert machinations may also be responsible for the serial mysterious deaths that have plagued the village in recent years.
The serial mysterious deaths are, without question, a revival of the traditional Watanagashi.

By performing the original Watanagashi on the same day as the modern festival -- which has degenerated into a mere village celebration -- they must be trying to remind the villagers of the sacred laws of Onigafuchi.
It is safe to say that investigating the Sonozaki family is the most direct path for research at this point.
If I can succeed in uncovering the secrets of the Furude Shrine's ritual storehouse, I intend to turn my research next to the Sonozaki family.
The Sonozaki estate is so heavily guarded that it is even protected by security cameras, but fortunately, I am acquainted with the twin sisters Mion and Shion -- Mion being the designated heir.
I would like to use this connection as a foothold for my next line of inquiry.
